helpful: Provisioned petition.txt with the full QP and argument text made the merits assessment possible without any fetching
statpack relist-count and per-Term cert tables gave a clean anchor for a zero-relist paid petition
gaps: The statpack lacks the 'Segment base rate by salience band' table the prompt describes, so no band-level anchor was available
A statpack cut for state-court vs federal-court originating forums on the modern cert slice would sharpen priors for petitions like this one
notes: fedcourts query has no free-text/topic filter usable for SCOTUS rows, so finding doctrinally similar priors (e.g. other summary-judgment constitutionality petitions) is not possible through the corpus surface; base rates carried the weight instead.
